questions.prompt: Sunflower seed-head spirals usually count to which numbers?
choices: Two consecutive Fibonacci numbers (like 21 and 34, or 55 and 89)
hints: Two consecutive Fibonacci numbers, often 21/34 or 55/89.
questions.explanation: Sunflower seed-head spirals (clockwise + counterclockwise) count to consecutive Fibonacci numbers like 21 & 34, or 55 & 89.

questions.promptTex: \text{The 'golden angle' that leaves rotate around a stem is approximately:} \\ \text{(answer in degrees, round to 1 decimal)}

questions.prompt: Why is $\phi$ optimal for leaf placement?
choices: It's the 'most irrational' number, so leaves never line up exactly — no shadowing.
It maximizes the height of the plant.
hints: What's bad: a rational angle → leaves line up periodically.
$\phi$ is irrational and worst-approximable by rationals.
questions.explanation: $\phi$ resists rational approximation best of all irrationals, so leaves placed at golden angles never line up — they spread out maximally over the stem.

questions.prompt: Drone honeybees develop from unfertilized eggs. How many parents does a drone have?
questions.explanation: Drones have only a mother (one parent), making their ancestor counts follow the Fibonacci sequence.

questions.promptTex: \text{A drone bee has 1 mother. The mother has 2 parents. Going back to generation 4, } \\ \text{ how many total ancestors?}
questions.explanation: Fibonacci! Drone ancestors per generation: 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13. Generation 4 has 5 ancestors.

steps.text: **Solution.** Fibonacci! Drone ancestors per generation: 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13. Generation 4 has 5 ancestors.

questions.prompt: A golden spiral grows by what factor per quarter turn?
hints: Each quarter-turn enlarges by $\phi$ (since the next square is $\phi$ times larger).
questions.explanation: Each quarter-turn of the golden spiral scales by $\phi$. Full turn scales by $\phi^4 \approx 6.85$.

questions.prompt: Which of these is a TRUE statement about $\phi$ in nature?
choices: Sunflower spirals often count to consecutive Fibonacci numbers, and many plants use the golden angle for leaf placement.
Da Vinci computed $\phi$ to 20 decimal places.
hints: Some claims about $\phi$ are over-stated; others are verifiable.
Plant spirals and phyllotaxis are well-documented.
questions.explanation: Plant spirals and phyllotaxis (leaf-placement) verifiably use Fibonacci/$\phi$ patterns. Many claims about ancient art are exaggerated.

questions.prompt: Romanesco broccoli is famous for its:
hints: Both Fibonacci spirals AND fractal self-similarity.
questions.explanation: Romanesco shows two mathematical features at once: Fibonacci-counted spirals AND a fractal-like self-similar floret structure. Lab-grown beauty.
